description: This figure displays a dual-axis line chart showing the calculated composition dependence of Poisson's ratio (δ, red squares, left axis) and the Pugh modulus ratio B/G (blue squares, right axis) for the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl alloy system across the full Mn substitution range (x = 0 to 1.0) based on first-principles DFT. Both parameters track each other closely with increasing Mn content, rising from the FeAl end member toward a pronounced maximum at the equiatomic Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al composition near x = 0.5, then decreasing toward a local minimum around x ≈ 0.75, before increasing sharply again at the MnAl end member (x = 1.0). The synchronous peaking of Poisson's ratio and B/G near x = 0.5 indicates the highest ductility and greatest compressibility in the intermediate composition range, whereas the low values at x = 0 reflect the most brittle behavior. The chart supports the conclusion that Mn content systematically modulates the elastic response and brittle-to-ductile character in B2-structured Fe-Mn-Al alloys.
